questions.q: In Hindu mythology, over which group of beings does Indra rule as king?
questions.explanation: Indra is celebrated in the Vedas and Puranas as the king of the devas, ruler of the heavenly realm of Svarga.

questions.q: What is the name of Indra's celebrated thunderbolt weapon?
questions.explanation: Indra wields the Vajra, the indestructible thunderbolt that the Rigveda celebrates as his foremost weapon.

questions.q: Which white elephant serves as the mount (vahana) of Indra?
questions.explanation: Indra rides the magnificent white elephant Airavata, the foremost of elephants who arose from the churning of the ocean.

questions.q: In which collection of texts is Indra the foremost and most invoked deity?
questions.explanation: Indra is the foremost deity of the Rigveda, the most ancient layer of Hindu scripture, where many hymns are addressed to him.

questions.q: Which heavenly realm does Indra rule as its king?
questions.explanation: Indra is the ruler of Svarga, the celestial heaven of the devas in Hindu cosmology.

questions.q: In Indra's central Vedic myth, which serpent does he slay to release the imprisoned waters?
questions.explanation: Indra's central Rigvedic myth is the slaying of the drought-serpent Vritra, freeing the waters that Vritra had withheld.

questions.q: Indra is principally associated with which natural phenomenon in the Vedas?
questions.explanation: Indra is the Vedic god of storm, thunder, and rain, who releases the waters and brings the monsoon.

questions.q: What is the name of Indra's resplendent heavenly capital city?
questions.explanation: Amaravati is the resplendent capital of Svarga, the heaven ruled by Indra, as described in the Puranas.

questions.q: In the Puranas, which young cowherd deity humbles Indra's pride at Govardhan?
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that Krishna lifted Mount Govardhan to shelter Vrindavan from Indra's storm, humbling the rain-god's pride.

questions.q: Indra is one of the principal deities of which broad category of gods in Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Indra is counted among the devas, the heavenly gods, and stands as their king.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, Indra's Vajra was forged from the bones of which self-sacrificing sage?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that the Vajra was forged from the spine of the sage Dadhichi, who gave up his body so the gods could make the weapon.

questions.q: In the Lifting of Govardhan episode, why does Indra unleash a devastating storm upon Vrindavan?
options: Because the cowherds worshipped Govardhan hill instead of him
Because Krishna stole the Parijata tree
Because the cowherds refused to pay tribute
Because his elephant Airavata was wounded
questions.explanation: In the Bhagavata Purana, Krishna persuaded the cowherds to worship Govardhan hill rather than Indra, prompting the angry rain-god's storm.

questions.q: What does Indra's weapon-name Vajra signify, beyond 'thunderbolt'?
questions.explanation: The name Vajra signifies both 'thunderbolt' (irresistible) and 'diamond' (unbreakable), expressing the weapon's indestructible nature.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, from what cosmic event did Indra's elephant Airavata arise?
options: The churning of the ocean of milk
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that Airavata, Indra's white elephant, arose from the Samudra Manthana, the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: Which divine wish-granting tree is most often named as standing in Indra's heaven?
questions.explanation: The Kalpavriksha, the wish-bestowing celestial tree that arose from the churning of the ocean, is said to stand in Indra's heaven, as told in the Puranas.

questions.q: In the Puranas, which deity's act of lifting a hill caused Indra to acknowledge his divinity at Govardhan?
questions.explanation: Krishna lifted Mount Govardhan on his little finger for seven days, after which Indra relented and acknowledged his divinity.

questions.q: Which guardian role does Indra hold among the directional deities (the Dikpalas)?
questions.explanation: Indra is the lokapala or guardian deity of the eastern direction in Hindu cosmology.

questions.q: In the Puranas, which celebrated pleasure garden belongs to Indra in his heaven?
questions.explanation: Nandana is the celestial pleasure garden of Indra within Amaravati, his heavenly capital, as described in the Puranas.

questions.q: Indra's thunderbolt Vajra is especially celebrated in the Rigveda for releasing what after a serpent was slain?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda celebrates the Vajra as the weapon that slew the serpent Vritra to release the imprisoned waters.

questions.q: Which sacred drink is closely associated with Indra in the hymns of the Rigveda?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda repeatedly associates Indra with Soma, the sacred ritual drink he consumes before his great deeds such as slaying Vritra.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, what is the name of Indra's consort, the queen of the devas?
questions.explanation: Indra's consort is Shachi, also known as Indrani, the queen of the devas in Hindu tradition.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which warrior son of Ravana earned the name Indrajit by defeating Indra?
questions.explanation: Ravana's son Meghanada earned the name Indrajit, meaning 'conqueror of Indra,' after vanquishing the king of the devas.

questions.q: In the Harivamsha and Bhagavata Purana, which celestial tree did Krishna bring from Indra's garden to Dvaraka, sparking a contest with Indra?
questions.explanation: Krishna brought the Parijata from Indra's heaven to Dvaraka to please his queen Satyabhama, sparking a celebrated contest with Indra.

questions.q: In the Rigveda, by what title is Indra often praised in connection with his slaying of the serpent Vritra?
questions.explanation: Indra is praised in the Rigveda as Vritrahan, 'the slayer of Vritra,' celebrating his defeat of the drought-serpent.

questions.q: In the Rigveda, which divine craftsman is named as the smith who fashioned Indra's thunderbolt Vajra?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda names Tvashtri as the divine craftsman who fashioned Indra's Vajra, the celestial smith of the gods.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, Indra is regarded as the divine father of which of the Pandava brothers?
questions.explanation: In the Mahabharata, Arjuna is born to Kunti through the boon of Indra and is regarded as the son of the king of the devas.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the title 'Indra' is understood to be which kind of designation?
options: A permanent name held by one eternal being
A title reserved only for sages
A position or office held in succession across cosmic ages
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Indra as an office or post of the king of the devas, held by successive beings across the cosmic manvantaras.

questions.q: In several Puranic narratives, Indra is depicted as feeling threatened by mortals performing what act, prompting him to send celestial dancers to disturb them?
questions.explanation: In Puranic lore, Indra fears that intense tapas by sages or kings may rival his throne, and he sends apsaras to disturb their penance.

questions.q: The celestial dancers who attend Indra's court in Svarga are most commonly known by which name?
questions.explanation: The apsaras are the celestial dancers and nymphs of Indra's heavenly court in Svarga, as described in the Puranas.

questions.q: In Hindu cosmology, what is the relationship between Indra's heaven of Svarga and the cycle of rebirth?
options: Souls who reach it attain final liberation and never return
It is a place of punishment for the wicked
Souls enjoy the fruits of merit there before returning to the cycle of rebirth
It is the abode of Vishnu beyond all worlds
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Svarga as the paradise where virtuous souls enjoy the fruits of their merit before returning to the cycle of rebirth.
